name: Icelandic Snowflake Breads (Laufabraud)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and sugar.
2. Mix in the melted butter until a stiff dough forms.
3. Turn out onto a lightly oiled surface and knead until smooth and cooled.
4. Divide the dough into four parts. Shape each into a ball.
5. Divide each into 4 parts to make 16 total.
6. Divide each of the resulting balls into 2 parts, for a total of 32 snowflake breads.
7. Cover baking sheets with waxed paper and dust lightly with flour.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each part of dough to form a thin round about 8 inches in diameter.
9. Place the rounds on the floured waxed paper. Chill for 30 minutes.
10. In a skillet, heat 2 inches of fat (375-400°F) over medium-high heat. Vegetable shortening, corn oil, or peanut oil can be used, but lard provides the authentic flavor.
11. Fold each dough round into quarters and make small cuts with a sharp-tipped knife to create cut-outs in the dough.
12. Fry the bread rounds until golden brown, about 1 minute on each side.
13. Remove from the fat and drain on paper towels. Store in an airtight container in a cool place or freeze until ready to serve.
